summary: Small duplicate-driven matching pass prompted by re-running `table-typer dups`. The automated report showed +2020 matched bytes, +0.05% overall matched code, 5 new 100% matches, and 3 partial improvements across fighter, game-mode, item, and stage code. The main reusable theme is using duplicate structure to replace hand-expanded logic with known helpers, split collision code into static inlines, and tune stack/register layout with `PAD_STACK` and `UNUSED` locals.

key_files.role: Major item collision rewrite: `it_802703E8` became an early-return/for-loop item-vs-fighter collision routine, while `it_802706D0` was split into static inline helpers for item-vs-item collision checks and damage handling.
key_files.match_result: Bot reported `it_802703E8` as a new 100% match (+36 bytes, 95.20% -> 100.00%) and `it_802706D0` as an improvement (+39 bytes, 93.39% -> 95.89%).

key_files.role: Rewrote total-frame calculation to sum all 0x19 `gmMainLib_8015D6F8` entries and return false if any `gmMainLib_8015D710` check fails, instead of returning after the first present entry.
key_files.match_result: Bot reported a new 100% match (+84 bytes, 78.60% -> 100.00%).

key_files.role: Adjusted Kirby Yoshi-hat dynamics setup: renamed the hat data local, used `PAD_STACK(2 * 4)`, introduced a scalar `bone_id`, used direct array access for dynamic descriptors, and wrote `FtPart_TopN` to `fp->dynamic_bone_sets[i].bone_id`.
key_files.match_result: Bot reported a new 100% match (+19 bytes, 92.98% -> 100.00%).

key_files.role: Collapsed duplicated animation-search code into a call to existing helper `grAnime_801C8318`, retained `PAD_STACK(8)`, and only checked `HSD_AObjGetFlags(aobj) & 0x04000000` locally.
key_files.match_result: Bot reported a new 100% match (+5 bytes, 97.92% -> 100.00%).

key_files.role: Added `PAD_STACK(8)` and replaced `random_adder_f`/`random_adder` with `rand_range`, including casts and argument order changes.
key_files.match_result: Bot reported a new 100% match (+5 bytes, 99.02% -> 100.00%).

key_files.role: Tweaked stack padding and control shape by removing the `ok` local, calling `it_80272C6C(gobj)` directly, using `PAD_STACK(12)`, and keeping a permuter-style empty `if (ip != NULL)` block.
key_files.match_result: Bot reported a partial improvement (+2 bytes, 98.80% -> 99.60%).

key_files.role: Added a padded variant of the hookshot position inline and moved repeated `Vec3` locals plus unused byte pads into the parent frame to influence stack layout in `it_802A4BFC`.
key_files.match_result: Bot reported a near-complete partial improvement (+1 byte, 99.90% -> 99.99%).

key_files.role: Mostly line wrapping, bracing, and expression-layout changes around Kirby special move code; the slice does not show a bot-reported match or improvement for these functions.
key_files.match_result: No Kirby function was listed in the decomp.dev progress comment.

key_files.role: Renamed a temporary pointer from `qwe` to register-flavored `temp_r31` while preserving the `lbTime_8000AFBC` timestamp write.
key_files.match_result: No bot-reported progress for this file in the slice.

what_changed: Applied a duplicate-report-driven matching sweep across 9 files, with 348 added and 412 deleted lines.

`it_802703E8` was rewritten from nested while/goto-style item hurtbox logic into early returns, `for` loops, and `continue` filters for owner, thrown-hitbox owner, team, hit capsule state, element, ground/air flags, and collision checks.

`it_802706D0` gained `static inline` helpers `it_802706D0_sub`, `it_802706D0_sub2`, and `it_802706D0_sub3` to separate active item hitbox preselection, item-hitbox collisions, inert toucher handling, damage accounting, direction selection, and special Pokémon SFX cases.
`gm_80163B9C` now accumulates total frames over 0x19 records before converting to hours/minutes/seconds/fraction fields; it returns false immediately when a required record is absent.
`ftCo_8009D81C` changed register/alias shape around Kirby hat dynamics by using a `hat` local, scalar `bone_id`, direct descriptor array access, and `fp->dynamic_bone_sets[i].bone_id` assignment.
`grAnime_801C84A4` was reduced to the same helper-call shape used by nearby animation flag logic: call `grAnime_801C8318`, pad the stack, then test one AObj flag bit.
`grKongo_801D55D8` switched to `rand_range` with explicit casts for one field and added stack padding.
`itLinkbomb_UnkMotion3_Anim` and `it_802A4BFC` received small stack/control-layout edits that improved but did not fully match the functions.

`ftKb_SpecialN.c` changes are mostly formatting and brace-placement churn in the provided diff; evidence for a functional or matching impact there is weak because the bot report did not list Kirby progress.
smart_moves: The PR reused Jelle's suggested workflow: rerun `table-typer dups` after more functions had matched, then harvest modest duplicate-driven wins.

Replacing the expanded `grAnime_801C84A4` body with a call to `grAnime_801C8318` is a clean duplicate match tactic: share the known animation search helper and leave only the differing flag bit check.
Splitting `it_802706D0` into `static inline` subroutines made a large collision function more matchable without introducing real calls, while isolating repeated hitbox and damage idioms.
The item collision rewrites prefer positive early filters and `continue` over deep nesting and gotos, which can better match compiler branch layout and is easier to audit.
The stack-sensitive Link hookshot change used explicit `UNUSED` byte pads, named `Vec3` temporaries, and a separate padded inline only for the branch that needed it.
The `ftCo_8009D81C` rewrite avoided holding a mutable descriptor pointer and instead used a scalar `bone_id`, reducing aliasing pressure and changing register allocation in a controlled way.
The stage Kongo fix recognized that canonical `rand_range` calls could replace local random-adder wrappers for a closer duplicate shape.
The PR kept questionable-looking permuter scaffolding, such as empty `if` blocks and `PAD_STACK`, localized to the functions where the bot showed remaining match pressure.
decomp_lessons: Rerun duplicate-finding tools after broad match progress; previously weak duplicate candidates can become actionable once more reference functions are matched.
When two functions duplicate most of their body, prefer calling the already-factored helper and preserving only the true local difference, as in `grAnime_801C84A4`.
Large collision routines can become more matchable by extracting repeated nested checks into `static inline` helpers while preserving exact side effects and loop order.
Stack frame shape mattered repeatedly in this PR: `PAD_STACK(16)`, `PAD_STACK(8)`, `PAD_STACK(12)`, `PAD_STACK(7 * 4)`, `PAD_STACK(2 * 4)`, and `UNUSED` pads all appear in matching edits.
Do not assume duplicate matching is only cosmetic; `gm_80163B9C` and `ftCo_8009D81C` include observable logic/assignment differences in the diff, so behavior must still be reviewed.

For owner/team collision gates, inversion from a large positive condition to several early `continue` cases should be checked carefully against flags like `xDCE_flag.b0`, `xDCE_flag.b1`, `xDCE_flag.b2`, and `xDCD_flag.b6/b7`.

Register-shaped local names and scalar temporaries can be acceptable when they document a matching constraint, but should be tied to evidence like `temp_r31` or `bone_id` rather than arbitrary placeholders.
Tiny improvements near 99% often come from stack-local lifetime and declaration placement, not algorithmic changes; `it_802A4BFC` improved by only +1 byte after padding/local-layout edits.
naming_conventions: `KirbyHatStruct* data` was renamed to the more specific `hat` in `ftCo_8009D81C`.
A descriptor field was pulled into a scalar local named `bone_id`, matching the field's meaning and avoiding repeated `desc->bone_id` use.
Register-flavored temporary names are used when matching dominates readability: `qwe` became `temp_r31` in `gmMainLib_8015D984`.
Repeated stack-position locals in `it_802A4BFC` use numbered names such as `pos0`, `pos1`, `pos2`, plus `frame_pad`, `pad0`, and `pad1` for explicit unused padding.

New helper names in `itcoll.c` are still address-derived and provisional (`it_802706D0_sub`, `_sub2`, `_sub3`), which is evidence of a matching-oriented extraction rather than finalized semantic naming.
The code continues project conventions for typed game objects and collision structs: `Item_GObj`, `Fighter_GObj`, `HSD_GObj`, `HitCapsule`, `HurtCapsule`, and `ItemKind`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use `table-typer dups` to find duplicate or near-duplicate functions worth reworking.
Tune stack layout with `PAD_STACK` expressions, including `PAD_STACK(2 * 4)`, `PAD_STACK(7 * 4)`, `PAD_STACK(8)`, `PAD_STACK(12)`, and `PAD_STACK(16)`.
Use `UNUSED` locals and byte arrays to force stack slots without semantic effects, as in `it_802A2EE4_inline_alt_pad` and `it_802A4BFC`.
Replace expanded duplicate logic with a helper call when an existing function already has the desired body shape, as with `grAnime_801C8318`.
Change loop structure from while/goto to `for` loops plus early `continue`/`break` to influence branch layout in collision code.
Introduce scalar temporaries such as `bone_id` to alter aliasing and register allocation.
Use exact constants and casts seen in the target shape, such as looping to `0x19` and calling `rand_range((s32) grKg_804D6980->unk30, (s32) grKg_804D6980->unk2C)`.
Keep small permuter scaffolding when it helps match, including empty `if` blocks and seemingly redundant non-null checks.
review_feedback: No human reviews or review comments are present in the PR slice.
The PR body credits Jelle with suggesting a rerun of `table-typer dups`; this is the only human guidance captured.
The author described the result as "modest, but real" and planned to try relaxing the duplicate metric next.
The decomp.dev bot reported matched code at 69.90%, +0.05%, +2020 bytes.
The bot listed 5 new matches: `gm_80163B9C`, `it_802703E8`, `ftCo_8009D81C`, `grAnime_801C84A4`, and `grKongo_801D55D8`.
follow_up_queries: Rerun `table-typer dups` after later match gains and compare new candidates against PR 2572's duplicate-driven edits.
Try the author's suggested relaxed duplicate metric for near-duplicates in item collision, Link item, and ground animation code.
Search for other `grAnime_801C8318`-style wrappers that can be reduced to helper call plus one `HSD_AObjGetFlags` bit test.
Continue investigating remaining mismatch in `it_802706D0`, especially helper boundaries, inert hitbox toucher handling, and stack padding.
Look for other uses of `random_adder` or `random_adder_f` in stage code that may match better as `rand_range` with explicit casts.
Audit high-99% functions like `it_802A4BFC` for stack-local lifetime differences, branch-specific padded inlines, and `UNUSED` frame arrays.
Review whether `ftKb_SpecialN.c` formatting-only churn was intentional or a byproduct of duplicate/permuter experimentation, since no bot progress was reported for Kirby functions.
